rulings.Case Title: SAMETC, LLC et al VS. TED AND PAULINE ELMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Prevailing Party And Fixing Attorneys Fees Recoverable As Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 12, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER SAMETC, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY AND FIXING ATTORNEYS FEES RECOVERABLE AS COSTS is continued to February 20, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FELL HOLDINGS LLC VS. FELL ST AUTOMOTIVE CLINIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Plaintiff And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 12, 2024 line 9. PLAINTIFF FELL HOLDINGS LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER SUBSTITUTING PLAINTIFF AND MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is GRANTED per CCP 368.5. The issues raised in opposition have already been addressed by this Court and will not be revisited. Additionally, it appears from the Court records that the prior order addressing this issue is currently on appeal. The Court agrees with the opposing party that the assignee stands in the shoes of the assignor and the new Plaintiff will in the end be bound by any final decision in this action as it pertains to the issues raised in opposition. Searles Valley Minerals Operations Inc. v. Ralph M. Parsons Service Co. (2011) 191 Cal. App. 4th 1394. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
